description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages (test and deploy) that sets the CARGO_HOME variable to $CI_PROJECT_DIR/.cargo and configures a per‑job cache (key ${CI_JOB_NAME}) for the target/ and .cargo/ directories with a pull‑push policy. In the test stage define three jobs: tests:nightly using the rustdocker/rust:nightly image and running the commands "cargo test --verbose --jobs 1" and "cargo test --examples --all-features --jobs 1"; tests:stable using rustdocker/rust:stable with the same two cargo test commands; and tests:semver using rustdocker/rust:nightly, allowing failure, with a before_script that runs "cargo +nightly install semverver" and a script that executes "./semver.sh". Add a pages job in the deploy stage that runs only on the master branch, uses the rustdocker/rust:stable image, runs "cargo doc", removes any existing public directory, creates a new public directory, copies the generated documentation from target/doc/* into public, and publishes the public/ folder as an artifact. Ensure each job inherits the defined cache and that the pages job’s artifact path is set to public. The pipeline should not use any additional services.
